title: Dactylorhiza × approximata
intro: Dactylorhiza × approximata, also known as the hybrid marsh orchid or intermediate marsh orchid, is a rare, terrestrial orchid species resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza sambucina and Dactylorhiza maculata. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in the treatment of various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × approximata include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal infusions and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into ointments and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × approximata is a hybrid orchid species, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. It is a cross between Dactylorhiza fuchsii and Dactylorhiza maculata. As a hybrid, it is not assigned a specific species classification under the binomial nomenclature system. However, it is often referred to as a hybrid of the two parent species. The classification of Dactylorhiza × approximata as a hybrid is widely accepted in the botanical community.
